creator: mi@FreeBSD.org
text:
The only thing, that makes my scenario special is that the disk was part of TWO redundant pools -- a RAIDZ1 (the first 3TB of it) and a mirror (the remaining 900GB).

As I replaced it, the machine rebooted unexpectedly and, upon returning, both pools remain UNAVAILABLE instead of being DEGRADED -- despite other pool-members all being healthy.

This prevents me from replace-ing the pieces of the replacement drive into each pool.

See the ServerFault link for more details (https://serverfault.com/questions/988835/).
